label: Finite State Machine
subject: How a finite state machine works
bossHook: Feed the string through the machine one symbol at a time, hop the token along the labeled arrow for each symbol, and halt on an accepting state — that run is the whole idea of an FSM.

vocab.hint: Read the input left to right. For each symbol, follow the arrow labeled with it out of the current state. Land on an accepting (double-ring) state and the string is accepted.

warmup.title: One Symbol, One Hop
warmup.hook: A machine sits in a state. Read the next symbol, find the arrow labeled with it, and move the token where it points. That single hop is all a state machine ever does.
warmup.winText: You followed the labeled arrow to the next state. Repeat that for every symbol and you're running the whole machine.

coin-flow.title: Follow the Labels
coin-flow.hook: Each symbol picks exactly one arrow out of the current state. Don't jump — take only the arrow whose label matches the symbol you just read.
coin-flow.winText: Every symbol took its matching arrow, and the token landed on an accepting state. That's an accepted string.

failText.wrong-transition: You moved the token down an arrow that doesn't match the symbol. Read the symbol first, then take only the arrow with that exact label.
failText.incomplete: You stopped before reading the whole input. The machine only decides once every symbol is consumed.

loop-back.title: Loops and Self-Arrows
loop-back.hook: Some arrows point back to a state you've already been in — even to itself. A repeated symbol just loops the token in place until a different symbol moves it on.
loop-back.winText: You let the token loop on matching symbols, then broke out on the right one and halted accepting. Loops are legal transitions too.

failText.wrong-transition: You skipped the loop-back arrow. When the symbol matches a self-loop, take it — the token stays put rather than jumping ahead.
failText.incomplete: Input left unread. Keep hopping — even through loops — until every symbol is gone.

boss-cycle.title: BOSS · Run the Whole String
boss-cycle.hook: A longer input, states wired with branches and loops. Feed it symbol by symbol, take the one matching arrow each time, and finish on an accepting state.
boss-cycle.winText: Full string consumed, every hop legal, halted accepting. You just executed a finite state machine end to end — that's exactly what a regex engine or lexer does under the hood.

failText.wrong-transition: One hop took an arrow the symbol didn't label. A deterministic machine has exactly one right arrow per symbol — follow it, don't guess.
failText.incomplete: You halted with symbols still unread. Accept or reject is decided only after the entire input is consumed.

reset-drill.title: SIDE-QUEST · One Clean Run
reset-drill.hook: Drill the core habit: three symbols, a simple chain of states. Read each symbol, take its labeled arrow, and land on the accepting state.
reset-drill.winText: Three symbols, three correct hops, halted accepting. Follow the label, consume it all — that's the whole loop.

failText.wrong-transition: That arrow's label didn't match the symbol. Match the label before you move.
failText.incomplete: You stopped short. Run every symbol before the machine decides.
